memoryArtifacts.headline: BACON FAMILY HISTORY FOLLOWS:
memoryArtifacts.description:
BACON FAMILY

14 February 2018



It is a reverend thing to see an ancient castle not in decay, 

— but how much more it is to behold an ancient noble family
which has stood against the waves and weat …

memoryArtifacts.headline: History of Sir John Bacon IV and Lady Helena Tillot, by Lyman De Platt
memoryArtifacts.description: Sir John Bacon IV was born about 1359, of Bradfield, Yorkshire, England [it also said "of Bradsfield, Norfolk, England;" but I believe this is an error], son of Sir John Bacon II and Helena Gedding. H …

zh.lifeSummary: 當John Bacon IV於1359年出生在英國諾福爾可布拉德費爾德時，他的父親John Bacon III40歲，母親Helena Gedding36歲。他於1371年與Julian Bardwell在英國素福爾可賀瑟特結婚。他去世於大約1397年在英國英格蘭素福爾可布拉費爾德享年39歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可布拉費爾德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Bacon IV于1359年出生在英国諾福爾可布拉德費爾德时，他的父亲John Bacon III40岁，母亲Helena Gedding36岁。他于1371年与Julian Bardwell在英国素福爾可賀瑟特结婚。他去世于大约1397年在英国英格兰素福爾可布拉費爾德享年39岁，埋葬在英国素福爾可布拉費爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Bacon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: from the Norman French personal name Bacun, derived from the ancient Germanic name Bac(c)o, Bahho, based on the element bag ‘(to) fight, (to) dispute’. The name was relatively common among the Normans in the form Bacus, of which the oblique case was Bacon.

English and French: from Middle English, Old French bacun, bacon ‘bacon’ (a word of ancient Germanic origin, akin to Back 3), probably a metonymic occupational name for a preparer and seller of cured pork.

History: Gilles Bacon from Normandy, France, is documented in Quebec City, QC, in 1647. — Michael Bacon from England arrived in Dedham, MA, in 1640. Nathanial Bacon, from Stratton, Cornwall, arrived in Barnstaple, MA, in 1639. Another Nathaniel Bacon (1647–76), from Friston Hall, Suffolk, emigrated to VA and settled at Curl's Neck on the James river.

variants.name: Bagg
meanings: from Middle English bagge ‘bag, pack, bundle’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of bags and sacks of various kinds, including wallets and purses. It might also have been used for a beggar, or for one who carried their belongings in a bag.
from the ancient Germanic personal name Bago (Bac(c)o, Bahho), from the root bag- ‘to fight’ (see Bacon 2).

variants.name: Back
meanings: English (Devon, Kent, Sussex, and Norfolk): from the Middle English personal name Bakke (Old English Bacca). It is of uncertain origin, but may have been a byname in the same sense as 3.
English: nickname from Middle English bakke ‘bat’, of uncertain application, perhaps a nickname for a person with poor eyesight, from the expression ‘blind as a bat’.

English: from Middle English bakke ‘back’ (Old English bæc), hence a nickname for someone with a hunched back or some other noticeable peculiarity of the back or spine, or a topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or ridge, or at the rear of a settlement.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When John Bacon IV was born in 1359, in Bradfield, Norfolk, England, his father, John Bacon III, was 40 and his mother, Helena Gedding, was 36. He married Julian Bardwell in 1371, in Hessett, Suffolk, England. He died about 1397, in Bramfield, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 39, and was buried in Bramfield, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Bacon IV est né en 1359, à Bradfield, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, John Bacon III, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Helena Gedding, avait 36 ans. Il a épousé Julian Bardwell en 1371, à Hessett, Suffolk, Angleterre. Il est décédé environ 1397, à Bramfield, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 39 ans, et a été enterré à Bramfield, Suffolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Bacon IV 1359 in Bradfield, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Bacon III 40 und seine Mutter Helena Gedding 36. Er hat 1371 in Hessett, Suffolk, England Julian Bardwell geheiratet. Er starb um 1397 im Alter von 39 Jahren in Bramfield, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Bramfield, Suffolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Bacon IV è nato il nel 1359 nel Bradfield, Norfolk, England, il suo padre, John Bacon III, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Helena Gedding, aveva 36 anni. Lui sposato Julian Bardwell nel 1371, nel Hessett, Suffolk, England. Morì circa 1397, nel Bramfield, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 39 anni, e fu sepolto a Bramfield, Suffolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: John Bacon IVが1359年にイギリスノーフォルクブラドフィールドで生まれた時，父John Bacon IIIは40歳，母Helena Geddingは36歳であった。1371年にイギリススフォルクヘセットでJulian Bardwellと結婚した。彼は約1397年にイギリスイギリススフォルクブラムフィールドで39歳で死去し，イギリススフォルクブラムフィールドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Bacon IV(이)가 1359년 영국 노르폴크 브래드필드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Bacon III(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Helena Gedding(은)는 36세였다. 그는 1371년 영국 수폴크 헤싯에서 Julian Bardwell(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 대략 1397년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 브램필드에서 39세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 브램필드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Bacon IV nasceu em 1359, em Bradfield, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Bacon III, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Helena Gedding, tinha 36 anos. Ele casou-se com Julian Bardwell em 1371, em Hessett, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1397, em Bramfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 39 anos, e foi sepultado em Bramfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Bacon IV родился в 1359 г. в местности брадфиелд, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени John Bacon III было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Helena Gedding, 36. Он женился на женщине по имени Julian Bardwell в 1371 г. в местности хессетт, суффолк, Англия. Он умер около 1397 г. в местности брамфиелд, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 39 лет и был похоронен в местности брамфиелд, суффолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Bacon IV nació en 1359, en Bradfield, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, John Bacon III, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Helena Gedding, tenía 36 años. Se casó con Julian Bardwell en 1371, en Hessett, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Murió aproximadamente 1397, en Bramfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 39 años, y fue sepultado en Bramfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra.
